name: Mimi's Kosher Dill Pickles
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 tablespoons Dill Seeds or Fresh Dill Heads
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the cucumbers and remove blossom ends.
2. Combine kosher salt, mustard seeds, black peppercorns, chopped garlic, dill seeds or fresh dill heads, water, and vinegar in a saucepan.
3. Heat to boiling.
4. Pack hot, sterilized jars with cucumbers, whole or spears, and top with a bay leaf and sprig of dill head if used instead of seeds.
5. Fill jar with brine, leaving 1/4 inch headspace.
6. Seal the jars.
7. Submerge jars in a boiling water bath for 10 minutes.
8. Turn off heat when all jars are in the canner. Ensure jars are covered with boiling water.
9. Remove from the water bath when 10 minutes have elapsed.
10. Ready to eat in 2-3 weeks. Yield: 2-3 quarts.
11. This recipe can also be used for green tomatoes, okra, or peppers.
Note: If you have hard water, using bottled or distilled water keeps the pickles from becoming soft.
